system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

RAS

ddtSchemes

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;

}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 4 7 3) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 38.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Both errors persist from all previous attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1) has inward-pointing faces` — face `4(0 4 7 3)`
2. **`log.pimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading failure from blockMesh failing

## Root Cause Analysis

The error `"Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ... has inward-pointing faces 4(0 4 7 3)"` means the vertex ordering in this hex block violates the right-hand rule. The face `(0 4 7 3)` is inward-pointing. This happens because vertices 0, 3, 4, 7 don't form a convex quadrilateral with outward normals.

All previous attempts to fix the blockMeshDict by reordering vertices or restructuring blocks have failed because the **fundamental vertex positions create geometric constraints** that make it impossible to satisfy the right-hand rule with the current vertex layout.

## DIFFERENT APPROACH: Use `dynamicFvMesh` with `displacementLaplacian` and `floatingPointManifold`? No — too complex.

**NEW STRATEGY: Use `snappyHexMesh` workflow instead of `blockMesh`**

Since the manual blockMesh approach keeps failing due to the L-shaped geometry being difficult to decompose into valid hex blocks, **switch to using `snappyHexMesh`** to generate the mesh from a `.stl` file. This is the standard industrial approach for complex L-shaped channels.

### Steps:

1. **Create `constant/triSurface/*.stl`** — Define the channel walls as an STL surface. The L-shaped channel can be modeled as a simple extrusion. Create a file `constant/triSurface/channel.stl` with vertices defining the L-channel cross-section extruded in z.

2. **Modify `system/blockMeshDict`** to serve as a base mesh for `snappyHexMesh`. Use a simple bounding box mesh that `snappyHexMesh` will carve down to match the STL:

```
vertices
(
    (-22 0 -0.6)    // 0
    (210 0 -0.6)    // 1
    (210 26 -0.6)   // 2
    (0 26 -0.6)     // 3
    (-22 0 0.6)     // 4
    (210 0 0.6)     // 5
    (210 26 0.6)    // 6
    (0 26 0.6)      // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (200 120 2) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (2 6)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1)
            (1 2)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3)
            (1 2)
            (4 7)
            (5 6)
        );
    }
);
```

3. **Add `system/fvScheme`** compatible with `snappyHexMesh` (copy from pitzDaily tutorial).

4. **Add `system/fvSolution`** (already present, keep as-is).

5. **Create `system/snappyHexMeshDict`** — This is the key file. It defines the STL surface, refinement zones, and mesh generation parameters. The dict should:
   - Read `constant/triSurface/channel.stl`
   - Define a `castellatedMesh` with the base mesh above
   - Add `refinementSurfaces` to refine near walls
   - Enable `meshMotion` if needed
   - Set `snap` to move vertices to the STL surface

6. **Add `constant/triSurface/channel.stl`** — Define the L-shaped channel walls as triangular facets. For example, the bottom wall goes from x=-20.6 to x=0 at y=0, the left wall from y=0 to y=25.4 at x=-20.6, the top wall from x=-20.6 to x=206 at y=25.4, and the right wall from y=0 to y=25.4 at x=206. The STL should include all four wall segments.

7. **Modify `system/controlDict`** — Add `dynamicFvMesh yes;` and `adjustTimeStep yes;` and `maxCo 0.5;` to accommodate the moving mesh approach.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Convert to a simple coarse base mesh (single block) serving as input to `snappyHexMesh`. Remove complex block definitions.

2. **`constant/triSurface/channel.stl`**: Create the STL file defining the L-shaped channel walls.

3. **`system/snappyHexMeshDict`**: Configure `snappyHexMesh` to read the STL, perform castellated mesh generation, and snap vertices to the surface.

4. **`system/controlDict`**: Enable `dynamicFvMesh` and add Courant number controls.

5. **After creating these files**, run `blockMesh` followed by `snappyHexMesh -overwrite`, then `pimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire file with a simple single-block mesh serving as base for snappyHexMesh; Set scale to 0.001; Vertices: 0=(-22 0 -0.6), 1=(210 0 -0.6), 2=(210 26 -0.6), 3=(0 26 -0.6), 4=(-22 0 0.6), 5=(210 0 0.6), 6=(210 26 0.6), 7=(0 26 0.6); Blocks: single hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) with grading (200 120 2) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Boundary: inlet type wall faces (0 4), outlet type wall faces (2 6), upperWall type wall faces (3 7), lowerWall type wall faces (0 1)(1 2), frontAndBack type empty faces (0 3)(1 2)(4 7)(5 6)'}, {'file': 'constant/triSurface/channel.stl', 'changes': 'Create new file with STL defining L-shaped channel walls; Vertex 0 at (-20.6 0 0), 1 at (0 0 0), 2 at (0 25.4 0), 3 at (-20.6 25.4 0), 4 at (-20.6 0 0.5), 5 at (0 0 0.5), 6 at (0 25.4 0.5), 7 at (-20.6 25.4 0.5), 8 at (206 0 0), 9 at (206 25.4 0), 10 at (206 0 0.5), 11 at (206 25.4 0.5); Define triangular facets for all internal walls: bottom wall from 0 to 1 at y=0, left wall from 0 to 3 at x=-20.6, top wall from 3 to 8 at y=25.4, right wall from 1 to 9 at x=206; Include both z=0 and z=0.5 faces so total 12 triangles forming the closed L-shaped boundary'}, {'file': 'system/snappyHexMeshDict', 'changes': 'Create new file with castellation from constant/triSurface/channel.stl; Use the same base mesh as blockMeshDict; Set maxLocalCells to 500000; Set maxCells to 1000000; Add refinementSurfaces to refine near walls with level 2; Enable meshMotion; Set snap to 1e-6; Set tolerance to 1e-6; Keep all boundary patches as they are in blockMeshDict'}, {'file': 'system/controlDict', 'changes': 'Add dynamicFvMesh yes after the application entry; Add adjustTimeStep yes before deltaT; Add maxCo 0.5 after adjustTimeStep'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
